INFS3822 Detailed Rubrics (Term 1, 2026) Assessment 1 Marking Rubric Criteria % High Distinction Distinction Credit Pass Fail (85-100) (75-84) (65-74) (50-64) (0-49) 1 Data Preparation (Structured 20% Data preparation decisions Data is prepared Data preparation steps Basic data preparation is Data preparation is Data) are clearly explained and correctly with clear are correctly applied and completed (e.g. handling incomplete, incorrect, explicitly linked to the return explanation of key described. missing values, preparing or poorly explained. Are preparation steps handled effectively. variables). appropriate, complete, and correctly applied? Score Range 6.0-7.0 5.5 5.0 3.5-4.5 0-3.0 Updated March 2026

2B Interpretation of Structured 5% Provides precise Accurately explains Outputs explained Describes outputs rather Outputs Outputs interpretation of each model outputs and generally correctly but than explaining what they misunderstood, model’s outputs and clearly what results suggest, interpretation lacks mean. incorrectly Are model outputs clearly and explains what results though depth or depth or clarity. interpreted, or not accurately interpreted? indicate about return precision may be meaningfully behaviour. slightly uneven. explained. 2C Comparison Between Models 5% Models clearly compared, Clear comparison Comparison present but Comparison brief or No meaningful with explanation of what provided with somewhat descriptive or superficial. comparison between Are the two models differences imply about reasonable limited in analytical models. meaningfully compared? return risk and model explanation of depth. behaviour. differences. 2D Interpretation & Use of 10% Clustering results coherently Clustering interpreted Clustering results Clustering present but Clustering missing, Clustering interpreted within business appropriately and included and discussed minimally interpreted or misinterpreted, or context and explicitly used linked to structured but largely descriptively. weakly linked. unrelated to analysis. Is clustering appropriately to support structured model analysis. interpreted and used as findings. supporting evidence? 3 Business Insights 30% The analytical brief clearly Business implications Business insights are Business insights are Business insights are links analytical results to are clearly derived generally relevant but basic and loosely unclear, unsupported, This criterion assesses how well specific business from the structured rely more on connected to the analysis. or missing. you integrate analytical implications (e.g. analysis, with summarising results than evidence into actionable, Recommendations are Little or no operational decisions, risk appropriate reference integrating evidence. business-focused insights. generic or only partially connection is made identification). to clustering evidence.
